title: World Cup 2026 Group A guide - fixtures, schedule, standings and odds for Mexico, South Africa, South Korea and Czech Republic
original_text: Group A sees co-hosts Mexico open the World Cup against South Africa in a repeat of the first game of the 2010 tournament, followed by matches against South Korea and Czech Republic. More World Cup group guides: Group B | Group C | Group D | Group E | Group F | Group G | Group H | Group I | Group J | Group K | Group L World Cup Group A fixtures All kick-off times are BST World Cup Group A table World Cup Group A supercomputer prediction World Cup Group A stats Mexico guide Mexico fixtures All kick-off times are BST Thursday June 11: Mexico 2-0 South Africa - Mexico City Stadium, Mexico Friday June 19: Mexico vs South Korea , kick-off 2am - Estadio Chivas, Mexico Thursday June 25: Czech Republic vs Mexico , kick-off 2am - Mexico City Stadium, Mexico squad Goalkeepers: Carlos Acevedo (Santos Laguna), Guillermo Ochoa (AEL Limassol), Raul Rangel (Chivas). Defenders: Edson Alvarez (Fenerbahce), Israel Reyes (America), Jesus Gallardo (Toluca), Johan Vasquez (Genoa), Jorge Sanchez (PAOK), Mateo Chavez (AZ Alkmaar). Midfielders: Alvaro Fidalgo (Real Betis), Brian Gutierrez (Chivas), Erik Lira (Cruz Azul), Gilberto Mora (Tijuana), Luis Romo (Chivas), Obed Vargas (Atletico Madrid), Orbelin Pineda (AEK). Forwards: Alexis Vega (Toluca), Armando Gonzalez (Chivas), César Huerta (Anderlecht), Guillermo Martinez (Pumas), Julian Quinones (Al Qadisiyah), Raul Jimenez (Fulham), Santiago Gimenez (AC Milan). Manager: Javier Aguirre Mexico kits VOTE! How far will Mexico go at the World Cup?
